storyTitle: As Insurance Agent, I want to perform account status and history viewing to achieve understanding of the account's current standing and lifecycle events

functional.title: Verify current account status is prominently displayed with color-coded badge at top of detail view
preconditions: Insurance Agent user is authenticated and logged into the system
At least one account exists in the database with status 'Active'
Agent has permission to view account details
Application database is accessible and responsive

steps.action: Navigate to the Account Management section from the main navigation menu
steps.expectedResult: Account Management page loads displaying a list of accounts

steps.action: Search for or select an account with 'Active' status from the account list
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view opens and loads successfully

steps.action: Observe the top section of the account detail view for the account status display
steps.expectedResult: Current account status 'Active' is prominently displayed at the top of the detail view as a color-coded badge (e.g., green badge for Active)

steps.action: Verify the badge is visually distinct with appropriate color coding and readable text
steps.expectedResult: The status badge has a clearly visible color (green for Active), readable font size, and is positioned prominently at the top of the view above other account details

steps.action: Verify the status text within the badge matches the actual account status stored in the database
steps.expectedResult: The displayed status text 'Active' matches the account_status field in the application database for this account

postconditions: Account detail view remains displayed with the status badge visible
No data modifications have occurred — this is a read-only operation
Agent session remains active and authenticated

functional.title: Verify color-coded badge displays correctly for each distinct account status type
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
Test accounts exist in the database with each possible status: Active, Suspended, Cancelled, Pending, and Reinstated
Agent has permission to view all test accounts

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management and open the account with status 'Active'
steps.expectedResult: Status badge displays 'Active' with a green color-coded indicator

steps.action: Navigate back to Account Management and open the account with status 'Suspended'
steps.expectedResult: Status badge displays 'Suspended' with a yellow or orange color-coded indicator

steps.action: Navigate back and open the account with status 'Cancelled'
steps.expectedResult: Status badge displays 'Cancelled' with a red color-coded indicator

steps.action: Navigate back and open the account with status 'Pending'
steps.expectedResult: Status badge displays 'Pending' with a blue or grey color-coded indicator

steps.action: Navigate back and open the account with status 'Reinstated'
steps.expectedResult: Status badge displays 'Reinstated' with an appropriate distinct color-coded indicator differentiating it from other statuses

steps.action: Compare all observed badge colors to confirm each status has a unique, distinguishable color
steps.expectedResult: Each account status type has a unique color-coded badge that is visually distinguishable from all other status types

postconditions: All status badges rendered correctly with distinct colors for each status type
No data was modified during the viewing operations
Agent remains on the last viewed account detail page

functional.title: Verify account history section displays chronological list of status changes with all required fields
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
A test account exists that has undergone at least 3 status changes (e.g., Pending → Active → Suspended → Active)
Each status change has a recorded date, previous status, new status, and reason in the database
Agent has permission to view account history

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management and open the test account with multiple status changes
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view loads with the current status badge displayed at the top

steps.action: Scroll down or navigate to the 'Account History' section of the detail view
steps.expectedResult: Account History section is visible and populated with a list of status change entries

steps.action: Verify each status change entry displays the 'Date' field
steps.expectedResult: Each entry in the history list shows a date/timestamp of when the status change occurred in a readable format (e.g., 'MM/DD/YYYY HH:MM AM/PM')

steps.action: Verify each status change entry displays the 'Previous Status' field
steps.expectedResult: Each entry shows the previous status value before the change (e.g., 'Pending', 'Active', 'Suspended')

steps.action: Verify each status change entry displays the 'New Status' field
steps.expectedResult: Each entry shows the new status value after the change (e.g., 'Active', 'Suspended', 'Active')

steps.action: Verify each status change entry displays the 'Reason' field
steps.expectedResult: Each entry shows the reason for the status change (e.g., 'Policy approved', 'Non-payment', 'Payment received')

steps.action: Verify the list is ordered chronologically with the most recent status change at the top (or bottom, per design)
steps.expectedResult: Status change entries are displayed in chronological order, and the sequence of previous/new statuses is logically consistent (e.g., the 'new status' of one entry matches the 'previous status' of the next)

postconditions: Account history section displays all status changes with complete data for each entry
The chronological order is verified and consistent
No data was modified during the read-only viewing operation

functional.title: Verify key account events (creation, endorsements, cancellations, reinstatements) are listed with timestamps and actor information
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
A test account exists with the following recorded events: account creation, at least one endorsement, a cancellation, and a reinstatement
Each event has associated timestamp and actor (user who performed the action) stored in the database
Agent has permission to view account events

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management and open the test account with multiple lifecycle events
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view loads successfully

steps.action: Navigate to the key account events section (may be within Account History or a separate 'Events' tab)
steps.expectedResult: Key account events section is visible and populated with event entries

steps.action: Locate the 'Account Creation' event entry and verify it displays the event type, timestamp, and actor
steps.expectedResult: Account Creation event is listed with event type 'Creation', a specific timestamp (e.g., '01/15/2024 09:30 AM'), and the actor name/ID who created the account

steps.action: Locate an 'Endorsement' event entry and verify it displays the event type, timestamp, and actor
steps.expectedResult: Endorsement event is listed with event type 'Endorsement', a specific timestamp, and the actor name/ID who processed the endorsement

steps.action: Locate the 'Cancellation' event entry and verify it displays the event type, timestamp, and actor
steps.expectedResult: Cancellation event is listed with event type 'Cancellation', a specific timestamp, and the actor name/ID who initiated the cancellation

steps.action: Locate the 'Reinstatement' event entry and verify it displays the event type, timestamp, and actor
steps.expectedResult: Reinstatement event is listed with event type 'Reinstatement', a specific timestamp, and the actor name/ID who processed the reinstatement

steps.action: Verify all events are listed in chronological order
steps.expectedResult: Events are ordered by timestamp from earliest to latest (or latest to earliest per design), and the sequence is logically consistent with the account lifecycle

postconditions: All key account events are displayed with complete timestamp and actor information
Events are in correct chronological order
No data was modified during the viewing operation
Agent session remains active

functional.title: Verify system correctly processes and returns account status and history data via API
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated with a valid session token
A test account with known status and history data exists in the application database
API endpoint for account status and history retrieval is deployed and accessible
Agent has appropriate API access permissions

steps.action: Send a GET request to the account status API endpoint (e.g., GET /api/accounts/{accountId}/status) with a valid account ID and authentication token
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 200 OK response with the current account status in the response body

steps.action: Validate the response payload contains the current status field matching the database value
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON includes a 'currentStatus' field with the correct status value (e.g., 'Active') matching the database record

steps.action: Send a GET request to the account history API endpoint (e.g., GET /api/accounts/{accountId}/history) with the same valid account ID
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 200 OK response with a chronological list of status changes and key events

steps.action: Validate the history response contains entries with date, previousStatus, newStatus, reason, and actor fields
steps.expectedResult: Each history entry in the response array contains all required fields: 'date' (ISO 8601 format), 'previousStatus', 'newStatus', 'reason', and 'actor'

steps.action: Verify the API response time is within SLA requirements (e.g., under 2 seconds)
steps.expectedResult: API response is received within the defined SLA threshold, confirming performance requirements are met

postconditions: API returned correct and complete data matching the database records
Response format and structure conform to the API specification
No data was modified by the GET requests
API performance met SLA requirements

functional.title: Verify account detail view loads correctly during an active customer interaction scenario
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
Agent is simulating a customer interaction scenario where they need to quickly look up account standing
The customer's account exists with a known status and history
System is under normal operational load

steps.action: From the main dashboard, use the account search functionality to search for the customer's account by account number or customer name
steps.expectedResult: Search results display matching account(s) within acceptable response time

steps.action: Click on the matching account from the search results to open the account detail view
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view opens and the current status badge is immediately visible at the top of the page without additional scrolling

steps.action: Quickly scan the status badge to determine the account's current standing
steps.expectedResult: The color-coded status badge (e.g., green 'Active') is prominently displayed and immediately communicates the account standing at a glance

steps.action: Scroll to the Account History section to review recent status changes and events
steps.expectedResult: Account History section loads with chronological entries showing recent status changes and key events with all required details (dates, statuses, reasons, actors)

steps.action: Verify the entire page load and data retrieval completes within an acceptable timeframe for a live customer interaction
steps.expectedResult: The complete account detail view including status and history loads within 3 seconds, suitable for real-time customer service interactions

postconditions: Agent was able to quickly determine account standing and review history
All data displayed is accurate and matches database records
Agent session remains active for continued customer interaction

functional.title: Verify account with only a creation event displays correctly with minimal history
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
A newly created test account exists with only one event (account creation) and no subsequent status changes
Agent has permission to view the account

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management and open the newly created test account
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view loads successfully

steps.action: Observe the status badge at the top of the detail view
steps.expectedResult: Status badge displays the initial status (e.g., 'Pending' or 'Active') with the appropriate color-coded indicator

steps.action: Navigate to the Account History section
steps.expectedResult: Account History section is visible and contains exactly one entry

steps.action: Verify the single history entry is the account creation event with timestamp and actor
steps.expectedResult: The entry shows event type 'Creation', the creation timestamp, and the actor who created the account. No status change history is shown since no changes have occurred

steps.action: Verify no empty state errors or broken UI elements are displayed
steps.expectedResult: The history section renders cleanly with the single entry, no error messages, and no placeholder text indicating missing data

postconditions: Account with minimal history displays correctly without UI issues
Single creation event is accurately represented
No errors logged in the system for this view

functional.title: Verify data persistence — status changes made to an account are reflected in the history view
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
A test account exists with status 'Active' and a known history of events
Agent or admin has the ability to trigger a status change on the account (e.g., suspend the account)
Agent has permission to view account details and history

steps.action: Navigate to the test account detail view and note the current status and number of history entries
steps.expectedResult: Account shows status 'Active' and the existing history entries are displayed

steps.action: Trigger a status change on the account (e.g., suspend the account with reason 'Non-payment') through the appropriate system action or admin tool
steps.expectedResult: System processes the status change and confirms the account is now 'Suspended'

steps.action: Refresh or re-navigate to the account detail view
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view reloads with updated data

steps.action: Verify the status badge now shows 'Suspended' with the appropriate color-coded indicator (e.g., yellow/orange)
steps.expectedResult: Status badge has changed from green 'Active' to yellow/orange 'Suspended'

steps.action: Navigate to the Account History section and verify a new entry has been added
steps.expectedResult: A new history entry appears at the top (or bottom) of the chronological list showing: Date = current date/time, Previous Status = 'Active', New Status = 'Suspended', Reason = 'Non-payment'

steps.action: Verify the actor information on the new history entry
steps.expectedResult: The actor field shows the name or ID of the user who performed the suspension action

postconditions: Account status has been updated to 'Suspended' in both the UI and the database
A new history entry has been persisted in the database with all required fields
The history maintains chronological integrity with all previous entries intact
Audit trail is complete with actor information recorded

functional.title: Verify account history displays multiple endorsement events with distinct details
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
A test account exists with at least 3 endorsement events recorded at different dates by different actors
Agent has permission to view account events

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management and open the test account with multiple endorsements
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view loads successfully with the current status badge displayed

steps.action: Navigate to the key account events section
steps.expectedResult: Events section is populated with multiple entries including endorsement events

steps.action: Count the number of endorsement events displayed
steps.expectedResult: At least 3 endorsement events are listed in the events section

steps.action: Verify each endorsement event has a unique timestamp
steps.expectedResult: Each endorsement event displays a distinct date/time, and no two endorsement events share the same timestamp

steps.action: Verify each endorsement event shows the actor who performed it
steps.expectedResult: Each endorsement event displays the actor name/ID, and different actors are shown where applicable

steps.action: Verify endorsement events are interspersed correctly in the chronological timeline with other event types
steps.expectedResult: Endorsement events appear in their correct chronological position relative to other events like status changes, and the overall timeline is logically consistent

postconditions: All endorsement events are displayed with complete and accurate details
Chronological ordering is maintained across all event types
No duplicate or missing endorsement events compared to database records

functional.title: Verify authenticated agent can access account status view with proper authorization
preconditions: Insurance Agent user account exists with the 'Insurance Agent' role and appropriate permissions
Agent credentials are valid and not expired
At least one account exists in the system for viewing

steps.action: Log in to the system using valid Insurance Agent credentials (e.g., username: 'agent.smith', password: valid password)
steps.expectedResult: Agent is successfully authenticated and redirected to the main dashboard

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management from the main navigation
steps.expectedResult: Account Management page loads and the agent can see the list of accounts they are authorized to view

steps.action: Select an account from the list to view its details
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view opens with the status badge and history section accessible

steps.action: Verify the agent can see the current status badge, status change history, and key account events
steps.expectedResult: All sections (status badge, status change history, key events) are visible and populated with data. No 'Access Denied' or permission error messages are displayed

postconditions: Agent successfully accessed account status and history information
Authentication and authorization checks passed without errors
Agent session remains active for further operations

functional.title: Verify account history handles reinstatement after cancellation with correct status chain
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
A test account exists that went through the lifecycle: Active → Cancelled → Reinstated → Active
All status changes have recorded reasons and actor information

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management and open the test account that was reinstated after cancellation
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view loads with the current status badge showing 'Active'

steps.action: Navigate to the Account History section
steps.expectedResult: History section displays the full status change chain

steps.action: Verify the cancellation entry shows Previous Status = 'Active', New Status = 'Cancelled', with a reason such as 'Customer requested cancellation'
steps.expectedResult: Cancellation status change entry is present with correct previous status, new status, reason, date, and actor

steps.action: Verify the reinstatement entry shows Previous Status = 'Cancelled', New Status = 'Reinstated' or 'Active', with a reason such as 'Customer requested reinstatement'
steps.expectedResult: Reinstatement status change entry is present with correct previous status, new status, reason, date, and actor

steps.action: Verify the key events section includes both a 'Cancellation' event and a 'Reinstatement' event with timestamps and actors
steps.expectedResult: Both cancellation and reinstatement are listed as key account events with accurate timestamps and actor information

steps.action: Verify the chronological order: cancellation event timestamp is before reinstatement event timestamp
steps.expectedResult: The cancellation event appears before the reinstatement event in the timeline, confirming correct chronological ordering

postconditions: Full lifecycle from Active → Cancelled → Reinstated is accurately represented in the history
Status chain is logically consistent with no gaps or contradictions
Current status badge reflects the final state of the account

functional.title: Verify UI layout and positioning of status badge and history section on account detail view
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
A test account with status and history data exists
Agent is using a standard desktop browser (e.g., Chrome, latest version)

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management and open the test account detail view
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view loads completely

steps.action: Verify the status badge is positioned at the top of the detail view, above other account information sections
steps.expectedResult: The color-coded status badge is the first or one of the first elements visible at the top of the detail view, without requiring scrolling

steps.action: Verify the Account History section is present and accessible on the same page or via a clearly labeled tab/section
steps.expectedResult: Account History section is clearly labeled and accessible, either visible on scroll or via a tab labeled 'History' or 'Account History'

steps.action: Verify the history entries are displayed in a structured list or table format with clear column headers for Date, Previous Status, New Status, Reason, and Actor
steps.expectedResult: History entries are displayed in a well-structured format with clearly labeled columns/fields, making it easy to scan and read during customer interactions

steps.action: Verify the key events section is distinguishable from the status change history or integrated clearly within it
steps.expectedResult: Key account events (creation, endorsements, cancellations, reinstatements) are either in a separate clearly labeled section or clearly marked within the unified history timeline

postconditions: UI layout meets design specifications with status badge prominently at top
History and events sections are well-organized and readable
Page renders correctly without layout issues or overlapping elements

negative.title: Verify system handles request for a non-existent account ID gracefully
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
No account exists in the database with ID '9999999999'
Agent has general account viewing permissions

steps.action: Attempt to navigate directly to the account detail view using a non-existent account ID (e.g., via URL: /accounts/9999999999/details)
steps.expectedResult: System does not crash or display a raw error/stack trace

steps.action: Observe the system response and displayed message
steps.expectedResult: System displays a user-friendly error message such as 'Account not found' or 'The requested account does not exist'

steps.action: Verify the error page or message provides navigation options to return to the account list or search
steps.expectedResult: A link or button is available to navigate back to Account Management or perform a new search

steps.action: Check the browser console and network tab for any unhandled exceptions
steps.expectedResult: No unhandled JavaScript exceptions in the console. API returns HTTP 404 Not Found with a structured error response body

postconditions: System handled the invalid account ID gracefully without crashing
User-friendly error message was displayed
Agent session remains active and the agent can continue using the system
Error is logged in the system logs for monitoring purposes

negative.title: Verify unauthenticated user cannot access account status and history
preconditions: User is not logged in (no active session or authentication token)
A valid account exists in the system
The account detail view URL is known

steps.action: Open a new browser session without logging in
steps.expectedResult: Browser opens with no active authentication session

steps.action: Attempt to navigate directly to the account detail view URL (e.g., /accounts/12345/details)
steps.expectedResult: System does not display the account status or history data

steps.action: Observe the system response
steps.expectedResult: System redirects the user to the login page or displays an 'Authentication Required' / 'Please log in' message

steps.action: Attempt to call the account status API directly without an authentication token (e.g., GET /api/accounts/12345/status with no Authorization header)
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized response with no account data in the response body

postconditions: No account data was exposed to the unauthenticated user
System enforced authentication requirements correctly
Login page or authentication prompt is displayed for the user to authenticate

negative.title: Verify unauthorized role cannot access account status and history view
preconditions: A user account exists with a role that does NOT have permission to view account details (e.g., 'Read-Only Auditor' or a role without account access)
The unauthorized user is authenticated and logged in
A valid account exists in the system

steps.action: Log in with the unauthorized user credentials
steps.expectedResult: User is authenticated and reaches the dashboard appropriate for their role

steps.action: Attempt to navigate to Account Management section
steps.expectedResult: Account Management menu item is either hidden or the user is denied access with a permission error

steps.action: If the menu is hidden, attempt to navigate directly via URL to the account detail view (e.g., /accounts/12345/details)
steps.expectedResult: System displays an 'Access Denied' or 'Insufficient Permissions' error message. No account status or history data is displayed

steps.action: Attempt to call the account status API with the unauthorized user's authentication token
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 403 Forbidden response with no account data in the response body

postconditions: No account data was exposed to the unauthorized user
System enforced role-based access control correctly
Unauthorized access attempt is logged in the system audit trail

negative.title: Verify system handles invalid account ID format in URL/API request
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
Agent has account viewing permissions
System expects numeric or specific format account IDs

steps.action: Navigate to the account detail view using an alphabetic string as account ID (e.g., /accounts/abcdef/details)
steps.expectedResult: System displays a user-friendly error message such as 'Invalid account identifier' and does not crash

steps.action: Navigate to the account detail view using special characters as account ID (e.g., /accounts/!@#$%^/details)
steps.expectedResult: System displays a validation error message and does not process the request. No server error or stack trace is shown

steps.action: Navigate to the account detail view using an extremely long string as account ID (e.g., /accounts/123456789012345678901234567890.../details — 500 characters)
steps.expectedResult: System rejects the request with an appropriate error message about invalid input length

steps.action: Attempt API call with SQL injection payload as account ID (e.g., GET /api/accounts/1 OR 1=1--/status)
steps.expectedResult: System sanitizes the input and returns HTTP 400 Bad Request or 404 Not Found. No SQL injection is executed and no unauthorized data is returned

steps.action: Navigate to the account detail view using a negative number as account ID (e.g., /accounts/-1/details)
steps.expectedResult: System displays a validation error or 'Account not found' message. No unexpected behavior occurs

postconditions: System handled all invalid ID formats gracefully without crashes or data exposure
Input validation prevented malicious payloads from being processed
Agent session remains active and functional
All invalid requests are logged for security monitoring

negative.title: Verify system handles database connectivity failure gracefully when loading account status
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
Database connectivity is simulated as unavailable (e.g., database service is stopped or network to database is blocked)
Agent attempts to view an account that would normally load successfully

steps.action: With database connectivity disrupted, navigate to Account Management and attempt to open an account detail view
steps.expectedResult: System does not display a raw database error, stack trace, or connection string

steps.action: Observe the error handling behavior on the UI
steps.expectedResult: System displays a user-friendly error message such as 'Unable to load account information. Please try again later.' or 'Service temporarily unavailable'

steps.action: Verify the error message does not expose any technical details (database name, server IP, connection strings)
steps.expectedResult: Error message is generic and user-friendly with no technical details exposed

steps.action: Restore database connectivity and attempt to load the account detail view again
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view loads successfully with the status badge and history section populated correctly after connectivity is restored

postconditions: System handled database failure gracefully without exposing sensitive technical information
Error was logged in the application logs with technical details for debugging
System recovered automatically once database connectivity was restored
Agent session remained active throughout the failure scenario

negative.title: Verify system handles expired session during account status viewing
preconditions: Insurance Agent was previously authenticated and logged in
Agent has the account detail view open with status and history displayed
Session timeout is configured (e.g., 30 minutes of inactivity)

steps.action: With the account detail view open, allow the session to expire by waiting beyond the session timeout period (or manually invalidate the session token)
steps.expectedResult: Session expires on the server side

steps.action: Attempt to interact with the account detail view (e.g., click to refresh history, navigate to another account, or scroll to load more events)
steps.expectedResult: System detects the expired session and does not return any new account data

steps.action: Observe the system response
steps.expectedResult: System redirects the agent to the login page or displays a session expired notification such as 'Your session has expired. Please log in again.'

steps.action: Log in again with valid credentials and navigate back to the same account
steps.expectedResult: Agent is re-authenticated and can access the account detail view with status and history displayed correctly

postconditions: Expired session was handled securely without exposing data
Agent was prompted to re-authenticate
After re-authentication, full functionality was restored
No data loss or corruption occurred due to the session expiration

negative.title: Verify system handles account with corrupted or missing history data
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
A test account exists where some history records have been deliberately corrupted or deleted from the database (e.g., missing 'reason' field, null 'previousStatus', or missing actor information)
Agent has permission to view the account

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management and open the test account with corrupted/incomplete history data
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view loads without crashing. The status badge displays the current status correctly

steps.action: Navigate to the Account History section
steps.expectedResult: History section loads and displays available entries. The system does not crash due to missing data fields

steps.action: Observe how entries with missing 'reason' field are displayed
steps.expectedResult: Entries with missing reason display a placeholder such as 'N/A', 'Not specified', or a dash '-' instead of showing a blank or causing an error

steps.action: Observe how entries with null 'previousStatus' are displayed
steps.expectedResult: Entries with null previous status display a placeholder or are handled gracefully (e.g., showing 'Unknown' or 'N/A')

steps.action: Observe how entries with missing actor information are displayed
steps.expectedResult: Entries with missing actor display a placeholder such as 'System' or 'Unknown' instead of showing blank or causing a rendering error

postconditions: System displayed available data gracefully despite corrupted/missing fields
No application crashes or unhandled exceptions occurred
Data integrity issues are logged for investigation
Agent was still able to view the account's current status and available history

negative.title: Verify system handles API timeout when retrieving account history for large datasets
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
A test account exists with an extremely large number of history entries (e.g., 10,000+ status changes and events) to simulate slow response
Network conditions are simulated to cause API timeout (or API timeout threshold is set very low for testing)

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management and attempt to open the test account with the extremely large history dataset
steps.expectedResult: System begins loading the account detail view

steps.action: Observe the loading behavior as the system attempts to retrieve the large history dataset
steps.expectedResult: A loading indicator (spinner, progress bar, or skeleton screen) is displayed while data is being fetched

steps.action: If the request times out, observe the error handling
steps.expectedResult: System displays a user-friendly timeout message such as 'Request timed out. Please try again.' rather than a raw timeout error or blank page

steps.action: Verify the current status badge still loads even if the history section times out
steps.expectedResult: If possible, the current status badge loads independently and is displayed even if the history section fails to load, providing partial functionality

steps.action: Click a 'Retry' or 'Try Again' button if available, or manually refresh the page
steps.expectedResult: System re-attempts to load the data and either succeeds or displays the timeout message again with retry options

postconditions: System handled the timeout gracefully without crashing
User-friendly error message was displayed with retry options
Agent session remained active and the agent can continue using other features
Timeout event is logged in the system for performance monitoring

negative.title: Verify system prevents XSS attacks through account history reason or actor fields
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in
A test account exists where a history entry's 'reason' field contains a script injection payload (e.g., '<script>alert("XSS")</script>') stored in the database
A test account exists where an actor name contains HTML injection (e.g., 'Hacker')

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management and open the test account with the XSS payload in the history reason field
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view loads without executing any JavaScript

steps.action: Navigate to the Account History section and locate the entry with the script injection in the reason field
steps.expectedResult: The reason field displays the raw text '<script>alert("XSS")</script>' as escaped/sanitized text, NOT as executable HTML/JavaScript. No alert dialog appears

steps.action: Locate the entry with HTML injection in the actor name field
steps.expectedResult: The actor field displays the raw text as escaped/sanitized content. No HTML is rendered (no bold text, no image loading). The text is displayed as plain text

steps.action: Inspect the page source/DOM to verify the injected content is properly HTML-encoded
steps.expectedResult: The injected content is HTML-encoded in the DOM (e.g., '<script>' instead of '<script>') confirming proper output encoding

postconditions: No XSS or HTML injection was executed in the browser
All user-supplied content is properly sanitized and encoded before rendering
System security is maintained against injection attacks
Agent session and data integrity are unaffected

negative.title: Verify system handles concurrent access to the same account detail view
preconditions: Two Insurance Agent users (Agent A and Agent B) are authenticated and logged in on separate browser sessions
Both agents have permission to view the same account
The account has existing status and history data

steps.action: Agent A navigates to Account Management and opens account ID 12345 detail view
steps.expectedResult: Agent A sees the account detail view with current status badge and history

steps.action: Simultaneously, Agent B navigates to Account Management and opens the same account ID 12345 detail view
steps.expectedResult: Agent B also sees the account detail view with the same current status badge and history. No locking or access denial occurs for a read-only view

steps.action: While both agents have the view open, a status change occurs on the account (e.g., account is suspended by an admin)
steps.expectedResult: The status change is processed and persisted in the database

steps.action: Agent A refreshes the account detail view
steps.expectedResult: Agent A sees the updated status badge (e.g., 'Suspended') and the new history entry reflecting the status change

steps.action: Agent B refreshes the account detail view
steps.expectedResult: Agent B also sees the updated status badge and the new history entry, confirming both agents see consistent, up-to-date data

postconditions: Both agents were able to view the account simultaneously without conflicts
After refresh, both agents see consistent and up-to-date data
No data corruption or race conditions occurred
Read-only viewing operations do not block each other

negative.title: Verify system handles account with no history events (empty history state)
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated and logged in

A test account exists in the database with a current status but with zero history entries (edge case where history records were purged or the account was created through a data migration without history)
Agent has permission to view the account

steps.action: Navigate to Account Management and open the test account with no history entries
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view loads without errors

steps.action: Verify the status badge is displayed at the top of the detail view
steps.expectedResult: Current status badge is displayed correctly with the appropriate color-coded indicator, even though there is no history

steps.action: Navigate to the Account History section
steps.expectedResult: Account History section loads without errors

steps.action: Observe the empty history state
steps.expectedResult: System displays a meaningful empty state message such as 'No history available for this account' or 'No events recorded'. No error messages, broken layouts, or 'null'/'undefined' text is displayed

steps.action: Verify the page layout remains intact and other sections are unaffected
steps.expectedResult: The rest of the account detail view renders correctly. The empty history section does not break the page layout or affect other UI components

postconditions: System handled the empty history state gracefully with an appropriate message
No application errors or UI rendering issues occurred
Agent can still view the current account status despite empty history
Agent session remains active for continued use

negative.title: Verify API returns proper error response for malformed request headers
preconditions: Insurance Agent is authenticated with a valid token
API endpoint for account status/history is accessible
API testing tool (e.g., Postman, curl) is available

steps.action: Send a GET request to /api/accounts/12345/status with an invalid Content-Type header (e.g., 'Content-Type: text/xml' when JSON is expected)
steps.expectedResult: API returns an appropriate HTTP error response (e.g., 415 Unsupported Media Type or processes the GET request normally since GET typically doesn't require Content-Type)

steps.action: Send a GET request to /api/accounts/12345/history with a malformed Authorization header (e.g., 'Authorization: InvalidTokenFormat')
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized with a structured error response indicating invalid authentication credentials

steps.action: Send a GET request to /api/accounts/12345/status with an expired authentication token
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized with a message indicating the token has expired

steps.action: Send a GET request with an Accept header requesting an unsupported format (e.g., 'Accept: application/xml' when only JSON is supported)
steps.expectedResult: API returns HTTP 406 Not Acceptable or returns the default JSON format with appropriate Content-Type header

postconditions: API handled all malformed requests with appropriate HTTP status codes
No sensitive data was leaked in error responses
Error responses follow a consistent structured format
All malformed requests are logged for security monitoring

edgeCases.title: Account with no status change history - only creation event exists
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials and has account viewing permissions
A test account exists that was just created and has never undergone any status change (only the initial 'Active' status from creation)
Agent is on the Account Search page
Database confirms the account has exactly one event record (creation) and zero status change records

steps.action: Enter the account number of the newly created account in the search field and click 'Search'
steps.expectedResult: The account detail view loads successfully without errors

steps.action: Observe the current account status badge at the top of the detail view
steps.expectedResult: The status badge displays 'Active' with the appropriate color-coded indicator (e.g., green badge)

steps.action: Scroll down to the 'Account History' section and review the status change list
steps.expectedResult: The status change history list is empty or displays a user-friendly message such as 'No status changes recorded' rather than an error or blank space

steps.action: Review the 'Key Account Events' section below the status change history
steps.expectedResult: A single creation event is listed with the correct timestamp, actor information (the user/system that created the account), and event type 'Account Created'

steps.action: Verify no loading spinners remain and the page is fully rendered
steps.expectedResult: All sections are fully loaded with no infinite spinners, broken layouts, or console errors

postconditions: The account detail view renders completely with all sections visible even when history data is minimal
No JavaScript errors or API errors are logged in the browser console
Agent remains on the account detail view and can navigate away normally

edgeCases.title: Account with extremely long status change history (100+ events)
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
A test account exists with over 100 status change events and 50+ key account events (endorsements, suspensions, reinstatements, cancellations, etc.)
Agent is on the Account Search page
Performance monitoring tools are available to measure page load time

steps.action: Search for the account with 100+ history events by entering its account number and clicking 'Search'
steps.expectedResult: The account detail view loads within the SLA-defined performance threshold (e.g., under 3 seconds)

steps.action: Observe the current account status badge at the top of the detail view
steps.expectedResult: The current status badge is prominently displayed and reflects the most recent status accurately with the correct color-coded indicator

steps.action: Scroll to the 'Account History' section and verify the chronological ordering of all status changes
steps.expectedResult: Status changes are listed in chronological order (most recent first or oldest first per design) with date, previous status, new status, and reason for each entry. Pagination or infinite scroll is available if the list exceeds the visible area

steps.action: If pagination is present, navigate to the last page of status change history
steps.expectedResult: The last page loads correctly, showing the earliest status change events with accurate data. Page navigation controls work without errors

steps.action: Scroll to the 'Key Account Events' section and verify all event types are displayed
steps.expectedResult: All event types (creation, endorsements, cancellations, reinstatements) are listed with timestamps and actor information. The list handles the large volume without truncation or data loss

steps.action: Measure the total page load time and memory usage in browser developer tools
steps.expectedResult: Page load time is within acceptable SLA limits. Memory usage does not spike excessively (no memory leaks). The browser remains responsive during scrolling and interaction

postconditions: All 100+ status change events and 50+ key account events are accessible and viewable by the agent
No data truncation or missing records compared to the database
Browser performance remains stable with no crashes or freezes
Agent can navigate away from the page without issues

edgeCases.title: Account with special characters and Unicode in status change reason fields
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
A test account exists with status change reasons containing special characters: ampersands (&), quotes ("), angle brackets (<>), Unicode characters (é, ñ, ü), and emojis (📋)
The account has at least 3 status changes with these special character reasons stored in the database
Agent is on the Account Search page

steps.action: Search for the test account by entering its account number and clicking 'Search'
steps.expectedResult: The account detail view loads without any rendering errors or XSS-related issues

steps.action: Navigate to the 'Account History' section and review the status change entries
steps.expectedResult: All status change entries are displayed with the special characters rendered correctly: '&' shows as ampersand, quotes display properly, angle brackets are escaped/rendered safely, Unicode characters (é, ñ, ü) display correctly

steps.action: Inspect the reason field containing the emoji character (📋)
steps.expectedResult: The emoji renders correctly in the reason field without being replaced by a placeholder box or question mark. Text alignment and row height are not broken by the emoji

steps.action: Right-click on a reason field with angle brackets and inspect the HTML source
steps.expectedResult: Angle brackets are properly HTML-encoded (e.g., < and >) to prevent XSS injection. No raw HTML is rendered from the reason text

steps.action: Check the 'Key Account Events' section for any events with special characters in actor names or descriptions
steps.expectedResult: Actor names and event descriptions with special characters display correctly without encoding artifacts or layout breaks

postconditions: All special characters and Unicode text render correctly throughout the account detail view
No XSS vulnerabilities are exposed through the reason or actor fields
Page layout remains intact with no visual distortion from special characters

edgeCases.title: Rapid consecutive navigation between multiple account detail views
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
At least 5 different accounts exist with varying statuses (Active, Suspended, Cancelled, Reinstated, Pending)
Agent is on the Account Search results page showing multiple accounts
Network conditions are normal

steps.action: Click on the first account (Active status) to open its detail view
steps.expectedResult: Account detail view loads showing 'Active' status badge in green with correct history

steps.action: Immediately click the browser back button and then quickly click on the second account (Cancelled status) within 1 second
steps.expectedResult: The second account's detail view loads correctly showing 'Cancelled' status badge (e.g., red) without displaying stale data from the first account

steps.action: Repeat the rapid back-and-forward navigation pattern for the third account (Suspended status) by clicking back and immediately selecting it
steps.expectedResult: The third account loads with 'Suspended' status badge (e.g., yellow/orange). No data from previous accounts bleeds into this view. The status history is specific to this account only

steps.action: Open the fourth account, and while the page is still loading (spinner visible), click the browser back button
steps.expectedResult: Navigation is handled gracefully. No partial data is displayed, no JavaScript errors occur, and the agent returns to the search results page cleanly

steps.action: Click on the fifth account and verify all sections load completely
steps.expectedResult: The account detail view loads fully with correct status badge, complete status change history, and all key account events. No residual data from any previously viewed account is present

postconditions: No race conditions or stale data displayed across rapid account switches
Browser history stack is clean and back/forward navigation works predictably
No orphaned API calls or memory leaks from cancelled requests

edgeCases.title: Account with simultaneous status changes by multiple agents
preconditions: Two Insurance Agent accounts (Agent A and Agent B) are logged in on separate browser sessions
Both agents have permissions to view and modify account status
A test account with 'Active' status is available
Both agents have the same account detail view open simultaneously

steps.action: Agent A views the account detail page and confirms the status badge shows 'Active' in green
steps.expectedResult: Agent A sees 'Active' status badge with the current history of events

steps.action: Agent B (in a separate session) triggers a status change on the same account from 'Active' to 'Suspended' with reason 'Non-payment'
steps.expectedResult: The status change is processed successfully in Agent B's session

steps.action: Agent A refreshes the account detail page by clicking the browser refresh button or a 'Refresh' control on the page
steps.expectedResult: Agent A's view updates to show the 'Suspended' status badge (e.g., yellow/orange) instead of 'Active'. The status change history now includes the new entry showing 'Active → Suspended' with reason 'Non-payment', timestamp, and Agent B's name as the actor

steps.action: Agent A reviews the Key Account Events section after refresh
steps.expectedResult: The suspension event appears in the events list with the correct timestamp, event type, and Agent B identified as the actor who performed the change

steps.action: Verify the chronological order of the history after the concurrent update
steps.expectedResult: The new status change entry is correctly positioned in chronological order relative to all previous entries. No duplicate entries exist

postconditions: Both agents see consistent and accurate account status after refresh
The status change history contains exactly one entry for the concurrent change (no duplicates)
Database integrity is maintained with correct timestamps and actor attribution
No data corruption from the concurrent access scenario

edgeCases.title: Account detail view when API response is slow or times out
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
Network throttling is enabled in browser developer tools to simulate slow connection (e.g., Slow 3G or custom 500ms+ latency)
A valid test account exists in the system
Agent is on the Account Search page

steps.action: Enable network throttling to 'Slow 3G' in browser developer tools, then search for the test account and click to open its detail view
steps.expectedResult: A loading indicator (spinner or skeleton screen) is displayed while the account data is being fetched. The page does not appear broken or blank

steps.action: Observe the page during the slow load - check if the status badge area, history section, and events section each show individual loading states
steps.expectedResult: Each section shows an appropriate loading state (spinner, skeleton, or placeholder text). The page layout is maintained and does not shift dramatically when data arrives

steps.action: Wait for the data to fully load and verify all sections populate correctly
steps.expectedResult: The status badge, status change history, and key account events all populate with correct data once the API responses arrive. No data is missing or corrupted due to the slow connection

steps.action: Set network throttling to 'Offline' mode and attempt to refresh the account detail page
steps.expectedResult: A clear, user-friendly error message is displayed (e.g., 'Unable to load account data. Please check your connection and try again.'). No raw error codes or stack traces are shown to the agent

steps.action: Restore network connectivity (disable throttling) and click a 'Retry' button or refresh the page
steps.expectedResult: The account detail view loads successfully with all data intact. The error message is dismissed and replaced with the actual account data

postconditions: The system handles slow and failed network conditions gracefully with appropriate user feedback
No data corruption or partial rendering occurs due to timeout scenarios
Agent can recover from network errors by retrying without needing to restart the workflow

edgeCases.title: Account with status change entries having identical timestamps
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
A test account exists with two or more status change events that share the exact same timestamp (e.g., batch processing created multiple entries at 2024-01-15 10:30:00.000)
Agent is on the Account Search page
Database has been verified to contain the duplicate-timestamp records

steps.action: Search for the test account and open its detail view
steps.expectedResult: The account detail view loads without errors

steps.action: Navigate to the 'Account History' section and locate the entries with identical timestamps
steps.expectedResult: Both status change entries are displayed individually - neither is hidden, merged, or overwritten. Each entry shows its own previous status, new status, and reason

steps.action: Verify the chronological ordering of the duplicate-timestamp entries
steps.expectedResult: The entries with identical timestamps are displayed in a consistent, deterministic order (e.g., by database insertion order or secondary sort key). The order does not change on page refresh

steps.action: Refresh the page 3 times and verify the order remains stable
steps.expectedResult: The order of the duplicate-timestamp entries remains identical across all 3 refreshes, confirming deterministic sorting

postconditions: All status change entries are visible regardless of timestamp collisions
Sort order is deterministic and stable across multiple page loads
No data loss or merging of records with identical timestamps

edgeCases.title: Account with extremely long reason text in status change history
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
A test account exists with a status change reason field containing 1000+ characters of text
Another status change on the same account has a reason field that is completely empty (null or empty string)
Agent is on the Account Search page

steps.action: Search for the test account and open its detail view
steps.expectedResult: The account detail view loads without layout breaking or horizontal scrollbar appearing

steps.action: Locate the status change entry with the 1000+ character reason in the Account History section
steps.expectedResult: The long reason text is either fully displayed with proper text wrapping or truncated with an ellipsis and a 'Show More' / 'View Full Reason' control. The text does not overflow its container or overlap adjacent elements

steps.action: If truncated, click the 'Show More' or expand control to view the full reason text
steps.expectedResult: The full 1000+ character reason text is displayed completely. The expansion is smooth and does not break the layout of surrounding history entries

steps.action: Locate the status change entry with the empty reason field
steps.expectedResult: The entry is displayed with a graceful placeholder such as 'No reason provided' or a dash (-) rather than showing 'null', 'undefined', or a blank space that collapses the row layout

steps.action: Resize the browser window to a narrow width (e.g., 768px) and verify both entries still display correctly
steps.expectedResult: Both the long-text entry and the empty-reason entry adapt to the narrower viewport without horizontal overflow, overlapping text, or broken layout

postconditions: Long text content is handled gracefully with wrapping or truncation controls
Empty/null reason fields display a user-friendly placeholder
Page layout remains intact across different viewport sizes

edgeCases.title: Account status view with browser zoom at 400% magnification
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
A test account with multiple status changes and events is available
Browser is set to default 100% zoom level initially
Agent has the account detail view open

steps.action: With the account detail view open, use Ctrl+Plus (Cmd+Plus on Mac) to zoom the browser to 200%
steps.expectedResult: The status badge, history list, and events section reflow properly. All text remains readable. No content is cut off or hidden behind other elements

steps.action: Continue zooming to 300% using Ctrl+Plus
steps.expectedResult: The layout adapts to the extreme zoom level. The status badge color-coded indicator is still visible and distinguishable. Scrolling allows access to all content

steps.action: Zoom to 400% and attempt to read the status change history entries
steps.expectedResult: Each history entry (date, previous status, new status, reason) remains accessible via scrolling. No text overlaps. Interactive elements remain clickable

steps.action: At 400% zoom, verify the current account status badge is still visible and identifiable
steps.expectedResult: The status badge text and color indicator are both visible. The badge does not overflow off-screen without the ability to scroll to it

steps.action: Reset zoom to 100% using Ctrl+0 (Cmd+0 on Mac)
steps.expectedResult: The page returns to normal layout immediately without requiring a refresh. All data remains intact and correctly displayed

postconditions: All account status and history information is accessible at extreme zoom levels
No content is permanently hidden or inaccessible at any zoom level
Layout returns to normal when zoom is reset

edgeCases.title: Account with all possible status transitions in history
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
A test account exists that has gone through every possible status transition: Created → Active → Suspended → Reinstated → Active → Cancelled → Reinstated → Active → Suspended → Cancelled
Each transition has a unique reason and was performed by different actors
Agent is on the Account Search page

steps.action: Search for the test account and open its detail view
steps.expectedResult: The account detail view loads showing the current (final) status with the correct color-coded badge

steps.action: Review the Account History section for completeness of all status transitions
steps.expectedResult: All status transitions are listed: Active→Suspended, Suspended→Reinstated, Reinstated→Active, Active→Cancelled, Cancelled→Reinstated, Reinstated→Active, Active→Suspended, Suspended→Cancelled. Each shows the correct previous status, new status, date, and reason

steps.action: Verify that each status change entry shows the correct actor who performed the change
steps.expectedResult: Each of the different actors is correctly attributed to their respective status change. No actor information is missing or incorrectly assigned

steps.action: Review the Key Account Events section for all corresponding events
steps.expectedResult: Events for creation, each endorsement, each cancellation, and each reinstatement are listed with correct timestamps and actor information. The event count matches the expected number of lifecycle events

steps.action: Verify the chronological order is strictly maintained from oldest to newest (or newest to oldest per design)
steps.expectedResult: All entries are in strict chronological order with no out-of-sequence entries. Timestamps progress logically without any entry appearing before its predecessor

postconditions: Complete lifecycle history is accurately represented in both the status change and events sections
All status types and transitions are correctly color-coded and labeled
Actor attribution is accurate across all entries
No missing or duplicate entries in the history

edgeCases.title: Account detail view accessed with an invalid or non-existent account ID in URL
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
Agent is on any page within the application
The account ID 'XXXXXXXXX999' does not exist in the database
The URL pattern for account detail view is known (e.g., /accounts/{accountId}/detail)

steps.action: Manually modify the browser URL to navigate to an account detail view with a non-existent account ID (e.g., /accounts/XXXXXXXXX999/detail) and press Enter
steps.expectedResult: The system displays a clear error message such as 'Account not found' or 'The requested account does not exist'. No blank page, no unhandled exception, no raw error stack trace

steps.action: Modify the URL to use special characters in the account ID (e.g., /accounts/<script>alert(1)</script>/detail) and press Enter
steps.expectedResult: The system rejects the invalid input safely. No script execution occurs. A user-friendly error message is displayed. The application remains secure and functional

steps.action: Modify the URL to use an extremely long string (500+ characters) as the account ID and press Enter
steps.expectedResult: The system handles the oversized input gracefully, either truncating it or returning a validation error. No server error (500) or application crash occurs

steps.action: Navigate back to the Account Search page using the application navigation
steps.expectedResult: The agent can navigate back to the search page normally. The application state is not corrupted by the invalid URL attempts

postconditions: No security vulnerabilities exposed through URL manipulation
Application remains stable and navigable after invalid URL attempts
No sensitive error information leaked to the user interface

edgeCases.title: Account history with status changes spanning multiple years and timezone boundaries
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
A test account exists with status changes spanning 5+ years, including changes that occurred at midnight UTC, on daylight saving time transition dates, and on December 31 / January 1 boundaries
Agent's local timezone is different from the server timezone (e.g., agent in PST, server in UTC)
Agent is on the Account Search page

steps.action: Search for the test account and open its detail view
steps.expectedResult: The account detail view loads with all historical data spanning multiple years

steps.action: Review the timestamps on status change entries that occurred at midnight UTC on a DST transition date
steps.expectedResult: Timestamps are displayed consistently in the agent's local timezone (or a clearly labeled timezone). The date is not off by one day due to timezone conversion errors

steps.action: Locate the status change that occurred on December 31, 2023 at 11:59 PM UTC and the one on January 1, 2024 at 12:01 AM UTC
steps.expectedResult: Both entries show the correct dates after timezone conversion. The year boundary is handled correctly - the Dec 31 entry does not show as Jan 1 or vice versa

steps.action: Verify that the chronological sort order is correct across the year boundary and DST transitions
steps.expectedResult: All entries maintain correct chronological order. No entries are out of sequence due to timezone conversion or DST offset changes

steps.action: Check the oldest entry (5+ years ago) for correct date formatting
steps.expectedResult: The date format is consistent with recent entries. No formatting issues with older dates (e.g., two-digit year display, missing century)

postconditions: All timestamps display correctly regardless of timezone differences and DST transitions
Chronological ordering is accurate across year boundaries and timezone changes
Date formatting is consistent across all historical entries

accessibility.title: Full keyboard navigation through account status and history sections
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
A test account with multiple status changes and key events is open in the detail view
Mouse/trackpad is not used - keyboard only
Browser is Chrome, Firefox, or Edge (latest version)

steps.action: Press Tab key repeatedly starting from the top of the account detail page to navigate through all interactive elements
steps.expectedResult: Focus moves in a logical, sequential order: first to the status badge area (if interactive), then through the Account History section entries, then through the Key Account Events section. Each focusable element receives a visible focus indicator

steps.action: Continue pressing Tab to navigate through any 'Show More', 'Expand', pagination, or filter controls in the history section
steps.expectedResult: All interactive controls (buttons, links, pagination controls) receive keyboard focus in a logical order. No interactive element is skipped

steps.action: Press Shift+Tab to navigate backwards through the same elements
steps.expectedResult: Focus moves in reverse order through all previously focused elements. The reverse order is the exact opposite of the forward Tab order

steps.action: If pagination exists in the history section, use Tab to focus on a page number and press Enter to navigate to that page
steps.expectedResult: The page changes to the selected page number. Focus is managed appropriately - either remaining on the pagination control or moving to the first item in the newly loaded list

steps.action: If any expandable/collapsible sections exist (e.g., event details), focus on the expand control and press Enter or Space
steps.expectedResult: The section expands/collapses. Focus remains on the control or moves to the newly revealed content. The expanded state is communicated to assistive technology

steps.action: Press Escape key while focused on any expanded section or modal
steps.expectedResult: The expanded section collapses or the modal closes. Focus returns to the trigger element that opened it

postconditions: All interactive elements on the account detail view are reachable via keyboard alone
No keyboard traps exist - the user can always Tab away from any element
Focus order follows a logical reading order consistent with the visual layout

accessibility.title: Screen reader announces account status badge with color-independent information
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
Screen reader is active (NVDA on Windows, VoiceOver on Mac, or JAWS)
A test account with 'Suspended' status is open in the detail view
Screen reader verbosity is set to default level

steps.action: Navigate to the account status badge at the top of the detail view using the screen reader's navigation commands (e.g., Tab or arrow keys)
steps.expectedResult: The screen reader announces the status text (e.g., 'Account Status: Suspended') along with any semantic role. The color of the badge is NOT the only way the status is communicated - the text label is sufficient on its own

steps.action: Use the screen reader's heading navigation (e.g., 'H' key in NVDA browse mode) to navigate through the page sections
steps.expectedResult: The page has proper heading hierarchy: the account title/name as a top-level heading, 'Account Status' as a section heading, 'Account History' as a section heading, and 'Key Account Events' as a section heading. Headings are in logical order (h1 → h2 → h3)

steps.action: Navigate to the Account History section and have the screen reader read the first status change entry
steps.expectedResult: The screen reader announces all relevant information: date of change, previous status, new status, and reason. The information is presented in a logical reading order

steps.action: Navigate to the Key Account Events section and have the screen reader read an event entry
steps.expectedResult: The screen reader announces the event type, timestamp, and actor information. Each piece of data is clearly labeled (e.g., 'Event: Endorsement, Date: January 15, 2024, Performed by: John Smith')

steps.action: Use the screen reader's landmark navigation (e.g., 'D' key in NVDA) to jump between page regions
steps.expectedResult: ARIA landmarks are defined for the main content area, navigation, and key sections. The screen reader can jump between landmarks efficiently

postconditions: All account status and history information is fully accessible via screen reader without visual reference
Status information is conveyed through text, not color alone
Page structure uses proper semantic HTML and ARIA landmarks

accessibility.title: Color-coded status badge meets WCAG 2.1 contrast requirements and does not rely on color alone
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
Accounts with each possible status are available: Active (green), Suspended (yellow/orange), Cancelled (red), Reinstated (blue or other)
A color contrast analyzer tool is available (e.g., WebAIM Contrast Checker, browser DevTools accessibility panel)
Agent has the account detail view open

steps.action: Open the account with 'Active' status and use the color contrast analyzer to measure the contrast ratio between the badge text color and the badge background color (green)
steps.expectedResult: The contrast ratio between the text and background of the Active badge meets WCAG 2.1 AA minimum: 4.5:1 for normal text or 3:1 for large text (18pt+ or 14pt+ bold)

steps.action: Repeat the contrast measurement for the 'Suspended' status badge (yellow/orange background)
steps.expectedResult: The Suspended badge meets the same WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio requirements. Yellow backgrounds are particularly checked as they often fail contrast with white text

steps.action: Repeat for 'Cancelled' (red) and any other status badge colors
steps.expectedResult: All status badge variants meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements

steps.action: Enable Windows High Contrast Mode (or equivalent OS accessibility setting) and view each status badge
steps.expectedResult: Status badges remain distinguishable in High Contrast Mode. The status text is readable and the badge boundaries are visible

steps.action: Verify that each status badge includes a text label in addition to the color indicator
steps.expectedResult: Each badge displays the status name as text (e.g., 'Active', 'Suspended', 'Cancelled'). An icon or pattern may also supplement the color, but at minimum, text is always present so color is never the sole means of conveying status

steps.action: Use a color blindness simulator (e.g., Chrome DevTools rendering emulation for protanopia, deuteranopia, tritanopia) to view all status badges
steps.expectedResult: Under all color blindness simulations, the status badges remain distinguishable from each other due to the text labels and/or supplementary visual indicators (icons, patterns). No two statuses appear identical

postconditions: All color-coded status badges meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements
Status information is conveyed through text and not color alone (WCAG 1.4.1)
Badges are distinguishable under all color vision deficiency simulations

accessibility.title: Account history table/list uses proper ARIA roles and labels for data structure
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
A test account with at least 5 status change entries is open in the detail view
Browser developer tools are available for DOM inspection
Screen reader (NVDA or VoiceOver) is active

steps.action: Open browser developer tools and inspect the HTML structure of the Account History section
steps.expectedResult: If the history is presented as a table, it uses proper <table>, <thead>, <tbody>, <tr>, <th>, and <td> elements. If it is a list, it uses <ul>/<ol> with <li> elements or appropriate ARIA roles (role='list', role='listitem'). Column headers or data labels are properly associated with data cells

steps.action: Check for aria-label or aria-labelledby on the history section container
steps.expectedResult: The history section has an accessible name via aria-label (e.g., 'Account Status Change History') or aria-labelledby pointing to the section heading. Screen readers can identify the purpose of this section

steps.action: Using the screen reader, navigate into the history table/list and read through the first entry
steps.expectedResult: The screen reader announces the table/list context (e.g., 'Table with 5 rows and 4 columns' or 'List with 5 items'). For tables, column headers are announced with each cell value (e.g., 'Date: January 15, 2024, Previous Status: Active, New Status: Suspended, Reason: Non-payment')

steps.action: Check the Key Account Events section for similar ARIA implementation
steps.expectedResult: The events section also has proper semantic structure, accessible name, and data associations. Each event entry's components (type, timestamp, actor) are properly labeled

steps.action: Verify that any dynamic content updates (e.g., loading new history entries via pagination or filtering) use aria-live regions
steps.expectedResult: When new content loads, an aria-live='polite' region announces the update (e.g., 'Showing entries 6-10 of 25' or 'History updated'). The screen reader user is informed of content changes without losing their current focus position

postconditions: All data structures use proper semantic HTML or ARIA roles
Screen readers can navigate and understand the data relationships in history and events sections
Dynamic content updates are announced via ARIA live regions

accessibility.title: Focus management when loading account detail view and during dynamic content updates
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
Agent is on the Account Search results page with multiple accounts listed
Screen reader is active
Keyboard-only navigation is being used

steps.action: Using keyboard, Tab to an account in the search results and press Enter to open its detail view
steps.expectedResult: Focus is moved to the account detail view - either to the page heading, the status badge, or the beginning of the main content area. Focus does not remain on the search results page or get lost at the top of the document

steps.action: Observe the visible focus indicator on the element that receives initial focus
steps.expectedResult: A clearly visible focus ring or outline is displayed on the focused element. The focus indicator has sufficient contrast (at least 3:1 against adjacent colors per WCAG 2.4.7)

steps.action: If the account detail view has a loading state, observe focus behavior during and after loading
steps.expectedResult: During loading, focus is either held on a loading indicator with an aria-live announcement ('Loading account details...') or managed so the user is informed of the loading state. After loading completes, focus moves to the main content or an announcement is made ('Account details loaded')

steps.action: If pagination exists in the history section, activate a page change via keyboard and observe focus management
steps.expectedResult: After the new page of history entries loads, focus is moved to the first entry in the new page or to the pagination control. Focus does not jump to the top of the page or get lost

steps.action: Tab through all elements on the page and verify no focus traps exist
steps.expectedResult: The user can Tab through all elements and eventually Tab past the last element on the page to the browser's address bar or next page section. At no point is the user trapped in a focus loop within a component

postconditions: Focus is managed predictably during page navigation and dynamic content updates
Visible focus indicators are present on all focusable elements
No focus traps exist anywhere on the account detail view

accessibility.title: Account detail view is usable at 200% browser zoom without loss of content or functionality
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
A test account with status changes and events is open in the detail view
Browser zoom is set to 100% initially
Browser window is at a standard desktop resolution (1920x1080)

steps.action: Zoom the browser to 200% using Ctrl+Plus (Cmd+Plus on Mac) or browser zoom settings
steps.expectedResult: The page reflows to accommodate the larger text and elements. No horizontal scrollbar appears for text content (WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow). All content is accessible via vertical scrolling only

steps.action: Verify the account status badge is fully visible and readable at 200% zoom
steps.expectedResult: The status badge text is fully visible, not truncated, and the color indicator is still present. The badge does not overlap other elements

steps.action: Scroll through the Account History section at 200% zoom
steps.expectedResult: All status change entries are fully readable. Date, previous status, new status, and reason fields are all visible without requiring horizontal scrolling. Text wraps appropriately within containers

steps.action: Verify all interactive elements (buttons, links, pagination) are still usable at 200% zoom
steps.expectedResult: All interactive elements have touch/click targets of at least 44x44 CSS pixels (WCAG 2.5.5). Buttons and links are not overlapping and can be activated individually

steps.action: Verify the Key Account Events section at 200% zoom
steps.expectedResult: Event entries with timestamps and actor information are fully readable. No content is clipped or hidden behind other elements

steps.action: Increase zoom to 400% and verify critical content remains accessible
steps.expectedResult: At 400% zoom, the status badge and history entries are still accessible via scrolling. The layout may switch to a single-column mobile-like view but all information remains available

postconditions: All content and functionality is accessible at 200% zoom without horizontal scrolling (WCAG 1.4.10)
Text remains readable and interactive elements remain usable at increased zoom levels
No content is permanently hidden or inaccessible at any tested zoom level

accessibility.title: Status change history timestamps use accessible date/time formatting
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
