A test account with multiple status changes across different dates and times is open in the detail view
Screen reader (NVDA or VoiceOver) is active

steps.action: Navigate to the Account History section and focus on the first status change entry's timestamp using the screen reader
steps.expectedResult: The screen reader announces the date and time in a human-readable format (e.g., 'January 15, 2024 at 2:30 PM') rather than a raw format like '2024-01-15T14:30:00Z' or Unix timestamp. If a relative format is used (e.g., '2 days ago'), the full date is available via a tooltip or aria-label

steps.action: If timestamps use a relative format (e.g., '3 hours ago'), hover over or focus on the timestamp to check for a tooltip with the absolute date
steps.expectedResult: A tooltip or title attribute shows the full absolute date and time. The aria-label on the element provides the full date for screen readers (e.g., aria-label='January 15, 2024 at 2:30 PM Eastern Time')

steps.action: Check the Key Account Events section timestamps with the screen reader
steps.expectedResult: Event timestamps are announced in the same accessible, human-readable format. Consistency is maintained between the history and events sections

steps.action: Verify that the datetime element uses the <time> HTML element with a machine-readable datetime attribute
steps.expectedResult: Timestamps use <time datetime='2024-01-15T14:30:00'> or equivalent semantic markup, allowing assistive technologies and search engines to parse the date correctly

steps.action: Check that timezone information is either displayed or consistently applied
steps.expectedResult: Timestamps either include timezone indication (e.g., 'EST', 'UTC') or a note on the page indicates the timezone used. Screen readers announce this context

postconditions: All timestamps are announced in human-readable format by screen readers
Semantic <time> elements are used for machine-readable date parsing
Timezone context is available to all users including screen reader users

accessibility.title: Error states in account detail view are accessible and announced to screen readers
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
Network conditions can be simulated (e.g., via browser DevTools throttling)
Screen reader is active
Agent is attempting to load an account detail view

steps.action: Simulate a network failure (set browser to offline mode) and attempt to load an account detail view
steps.expectedResult: An error message is displayed on screen. The error message container has role='alert' or aria-live='assertive' so the screen reader immediately announces the error without requiring the user to navigate to it

steps.action: Listen to the screen reader announcement of the error
steps.expectedResult: The screen reader announces the full error message text (e.g., 'Error: Unable to load account details. Please check your connection and try again.'). The announcement is clear and actionable

steps.action: Verify the error message is not conveyed by color alone (e.g., red text only)
steps.expectedResult: The error message includes an error icon with alt text, a text prefix like 'Error:' or 'Warning:', and/or is contained in a visually distinct container. The error is identifiable without relying on color perception

steps.action: If a 'Retry' button is present in the error state, verify it is keyboard accessible
steps.expectedResult: The 'Retry' button receives keyboard focus via Tab and can be activated with Enter or Space. The button has an accessible name (e.g., 'Retry loading account details')

steps.action: Activate the 'Retry' button and observe focus management after successful load
steps.expectedResult: After successful retry, focus moves to the main content area or the status badge. An aria-live announcement confirms success (e.g., 'Account details loaded successfully'). The error message is removed from the DOM or hidden with aria-hidden='true'

postconditions: Error states are announced immediately to screen reader users via ARIA live regions
Error messages are perceivable without relying on color alone
Recovery actions (Retry) are keyboard accessible with proper focus management

accessibility.title: Mobile touch accessibility for account status and history viewing
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in on a mobile device (iOS or Android) or mobile emulator
VoiceOver (iOS) or TalkBack (Android) screen reader is enabled
A test account with status changes and events is available
Device is in portrait orientation

steps.action: Navigate to the account detail view and swipe right through the page elements using VoiceOver/TalkBack gestures
steps.expectedResult: The screen reader navigates through elements in a logical order: account header, status badge, history section heading, history entries, events section heading, event entries. Each element is announced with its role and content

steps.action: Double-tap on any interactive element (e.g., expand button, pagination control) identified by the screen reader
steps.expectedResult: The interactive element is activated. The action is confirmed by a screen reader announcement. Touch target is at least 44x44 CSS pixels (WCAG 2.5.5)

steps.action: Verify the status badge is accessible via touch - tap on it with VoiceOver/TalkBack active
steps.expectedResult: The screen reader announces the status text and any additional context (e.g., 'Active, status badge'). The touch target for the badge is large enough to be easily tapped

steps.action: Rotate the device to landscape orientation and verify the layout adapts
steps.expectedResult: The account detail view adapts to landscape orientation. All content remains accessible. No content is cut off or overlapping. Screen reader navigation order remains logical

steps.action: Pinch-to-zoom on the account history section
steps.expectedResult: Zoom is not disabled (no maximum-scale=1.0 or user-scalable=no in viewport meta tag per WCAG 1.4.4). Content can be zoomed and remains readable

postconditions: All account status and history content is accessible via mobile screen readers
Touch targets meet minimum size requirements (44x44 CSS pixels)
Pinch-to-zoom is not disabled, allowing users to magnify content

accessibility.title: Account history list supports reduced motion preferences
preconditions: Insurance Agent is logged in with valid credentials
Operating system is configured with 'Reduce Motion' / 'Reduce Animations' accessibility setting enabled
A test account with status changes is open in the detail view
The account detail view includes animations (e.g., fade-in of history entries, slide transitions for pagination, loading spinner animations)

steps.action: With 'Reduce Motion' enabled in OS settings, load the account detail view and observe any animations
steps.expectedResult: Animations are either removed entirely or replaced with simple opacity transitions. No sliding, bouncing, or parallax effects occur. The CSS respects prefers-reduced-motion: reduce media query

steps.action: If pagination exists, navigate to the next page and observe the transition
steps.expectedResult: The page content changes without animated transitions. New content appears immediately or with a simple fade. No sliding or scrolling animations occur

steps.action: Observe the loading spinner/indicator behavior
steps.expectedResult: If a loading spinner is used, it is either replaced with a static 'Loading...' text indicator or uses a very subtle animation. Rapidly spinning or flashing indicators are avoided

steps.action: Disable 'Reduce Motion' in OS settings and reload the page
steps.expectedResult: Normal animations resume (if any were designed). The page correctly detects the change in motion preference

postconditions: The application respects the prefers-reduced-motion media query
Users with vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity can use the feature comfortably
All functionality remains available regardless of motion preference setting

userStoryObject.title: As Insurance Agent, I want to perform account status and history viewing to achieve understanding of the account's current standing and lifecycle events
userStoryObject.description:
CONTEXT & BACKGROUND:
Agents need to see the current account status (active, suspended, cancelled, etc.) along with a chronological history of status changes and key account events for context during customer interactions.

CURRENT STATE:
Manual or incomplete process today.

DESIRED STATE:
System supports the capability end-to-end.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
• Implement core logic and validation
• Expose API and UI
• Handle errors and edge cases
• Ensure data persistence and retrieval

USER INTERACTION FLOW:
1. User accesses the feature
2. User performs the required action
3. System processes the request
4. System displays results or confirmation
5. User verifies the outcome

TECHNICAL CONSIDERATIONS:
• Data source: application database
• Security: authentication and input validation
• Performance: meet SLA requirements

OUT OF SCOPE:
• Advanced customization (future story)

SUCCESS METRICS:
• Feature works per acceptance criteria
• No critical defects in production

**Persona:** Insurance Agent

**Acceptance Criteria:**
1. Current account status is prominently displayed with visual indicator (color-coded badge) at the top of the detail view
2. Account history section shows chronological list of status changes with date, previous status, new status, and reason
3. Key account events such as creation, endorsements, cancellations, and reinstatements are listed with timestamps and actor information
4. System handles the operation correctly and returns expected results for scenario 4
5. System handles the operation correctly and returns expected results for scenario 5
